pages.full_text:
__Thu Aug 12th__ Simon dropped us off in the middle of Edinburgh before he parked at Louise's. We looked around the middle of town & went to Louise's for a lasagna lunch. It was lovely to see Purdie + Finnian again, & meet Coralie. After lunch we saw Miles' shop. He is opening a new show room. To have one wall tiled has cost £5,000. Lovely terracotta. The Original Tile Company; Frederick St.

	After lunch we saw a show about a man-eating plant. In the evening SWP E saw a show about a man-eating plant, while I took J+C to "Star Wars."

	We had supper in the van, Purdie & Finnian came out to join us & had crisps & ½ an eccles cake. They are absolutely gorgeous & well-behaved. Tonic spent the afternoon in the garden & the evening in the van.

	William was pick-pocketed: T's mobile phone was stolen out of Wm's pocket. So Wm cdn't contact us on Simon's phone no, or find his way to Oxford Terrace, or remember the Nelson's [?surname]. But he remembered the name of the show & [?looked it up in the paper &] walked to the theatre.

__Fri Aug 13th__
	When I walked the dog in the morning I saw the remains of an old mine which Miles had mentioned. The pump is in an old stone house. The rest is red
pages.notes: Date heading 'Thu Aug 12th' is underlined. 'Fri Aug 13th' heading is also underlined. Year not stated on this page — within volume A (1989–2003), Aug 12 falls on a Thursday in 1993 and 1999; context (mobile phones mentioned, Star Wars film) could fit late 1990s/early 2000s. Annotation in smaller writing appears above/between lines near 'looked it up in the paper' — partially illegible. 'SWP E' reading uncertain — possibly initials for family members. 'Louise' could also be spelled 'Louise' — written as 'Louisa' in some places; transcribed as written each time. The phrase after Nelson's surname is a later annotation in smaller writing in the margin/between lines: transcribed as best read.

pages.full_text:
brick.
	Simon dropped E J+C off near Florence Shanks in Moray Place. T+ [?] wandered. S+W went to look at Edinburgh University & met a politics teacher.

	Florence & Jimmy were well & welcoming. We then went on to shop. The final shop was a fascinating pawn brokers. [?John told] Simon to tell him he was in [?Miles'] shop. Simon pick us up on Queen's [?+fred?]. We drove on back to Musselborough & after lunch at the side of the road we had huge pots of delicious ice cream.

	We reached North Berwick, & the Torrance's house "Sea Spray" on a cliff with a lovely sandy beach & a [?stunning] view across to the farm camp site at Anstruther. We had a cup of tea & wandered along the beach, climbing on the rocks, collecting cowrie shells & enjoying the beach. [?Dead birds?] Simon & Morag collected rubbish — plastic bottles — in black sacks. The boys were disgusted when Tonic tried to mate with another border dog. He finally got in a fight. Charles spent [?hours] & hours digging a sandcastle dungeon [?prison]. William & Simon left to put up the tent, & came back. Morag gave us a banquet supper. Tonic was very interested in Morag's dachshund, the 2 cats. Hew & Robun [?] with us. Tho
pages.notes: This page continues directly from the left page (begins with 'brick.' completing the sentence about 'red brick'). 'Musselborough' is the diary's spelling — likely Musselburgh. 'Florence Shanks' — could be a shop name or a person's name/address. 'Moray Place' is a street in Edinburgh New Town. 'Anstruther' — the campsite described seems to be visible from North Berwick across the Firth of Forth; geographically this is unusual as Anstruther is in Fife. Could also read as 'Anstruther' being the name of a local farm. 'Hew & Robun' — names partially legible; 'Robun' uncertain, could be 'Robin'. A handwritten page number appears to be partially visible in the top-right corner but is cropped/unclear — could be a '2' but may be part of a larger number or a chapter/section marker; recorded as null. The right-hand page text is cut off at the bottom.
